_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Create prototypes, wireframes, and storyboards based on customer requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Build and deliver AI proof-of-concepts for high-priority use cases, documenting technical approach, results, limitations, and production readiness assessment.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-020 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-A-004 (DQ, cosine: 0.457), DA-S-004 (DA, cosine: 0.445).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.485). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [based, create, customer, prototypes, requirements, storyboards] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, approach, assessment, build, cases, concepts] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.892, R=0.875). Balanced P/R — the 9-word DCWF element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Create prototypes, wireframes, and storyboards based on customer requirements." and "Build and deliver AI proof-of-concepts for high-priority use cases, documenting" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.458 ≈ STS 0.485 (gap 0.027). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.6%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [based, create, customer, prototypes, requirements, storyboards]. WIDAI terms: [ai, approach, assessment, build, cases, concepts].
